summary: The unit test suite covers all core requirements well: manifest parsing (package.json and requirements.txt), license classification, compliance report generation, and error handling. Both parser.test.ts and checker.test.ts are clearly organized with descriptive names, logically grouped describe blocks, and inline comments that reinforce the TDD narrative. Fixtures and mocks are minimal but purposeful — the inline mockLookup in checker.test.ts is clean and deterministic. Edge cases are handled notably well: invalid JSON, empty dependency lists, packages without version specifiers, comment/blank-line skipping, and lookup failures that must degrade gracefully. Case-insensitive matching is explicitly tested. Minor gaps: no test directly exercises formatReport (report.ts is untested in the unit suite), and the requirements.txt parser isn't tested with >= or ~= operators despite the implementation claiming to support them. The test-harness.ts integration layer adds strong end-to-end coverage via act, asserting exact output strings and exit codes, which compensates for some unit-level gaps. Overall a well-designed, trustworthy suite that would catch most regressions.
